evals.prompt: Calculate the total Delta-V required for a Hohmann transfer from a 500 km circular parking orbit (inclination = 28.5 deg) to a Geosynchronous Equatorial Orbit (GEO, 35786 km altitude, 0 deg inclination). Include the plane change at apogee. Assume standard Earth mu.
evals.expected_output: A step-by-step calculation showing v1, v2, plane change delta-v, and total delta-v, resulting in approx 4.3 km/s.

evals.prompt: For a circular LEO orbit at 600 km altitude, calculate the orbital period and the maximum possible eclipse duration (Umbra) assuming a cylindrical Earth shadow model. Use R_earth = 6378 km.
evals.expected_output: Calculation of period (~96.7 mins) and max eclipse duration (~35-36 mins) with clear working.

evals.prompt: A satellite is in a circular orbit with a semi-major axis of 7000 km. Calculate its nodal period and the longitude shift of the ground track per orbit (in degrees West).
evals.expected_output: Period calculation (~97.4 mins) and ground track shift (~24.4 degrees West) calculation.
